supersedes: spec/address-book-spec.json (v1, 2026-07-14) — carried where the rewrite preserved a ruling, superseded where it inverted one. See conflicts-v2.candidate.json for the per-ruling disposition.

pendingDisposition.EUDPA-185: STALE/SUPERSEDED — its scope (nav + list + pagination) is absorbed into EUDPA-287 (AC3 nav, AC4 list, AC6 pagination). Its own text was NOT rewritten (still 'operators', 'type' column, per-user, Priority Lowest). Recommend closing as a duplicate of EUDPA-287; confirm with the team. Its ACs map to 287 equivalents in acCoverage.
pendingDisposition.EUDPA-187: Empty/parked, unchanged. No scope to ingest.

relatedNotChildren.EUDPA-198: Standardise address format across FE/BE (replace addressLine3 with postcode). EUDPA-294 says mark it a DUPLICATE and ABSORB it. Model extension is ALREADY realised on feat/EUDPA-58-address-book (Address.java has county:18 + postcode:19; Operator/Transporter carry telephone+email) — the only outstanding 198 delta is DROP addressLine3 (Address.java:16 still present). Tracked under journeyIntegration ji-198-model + cv-029.
relatedNotChildren.EUDPA-142: Routing traders into a journey frontend (INS front-door concern). Out of scope of the address book itself.
relatedNotChildren.EUDPA-119: Approved commercial transporter list. Blocks the DEFERRED transporter address type; nothing here handles transporters (cv-013).
relatedNotChildren.EUDPA-59: Authentication / Defra ID — supplies orgId (currentRelationshipId) in the session that scopes every read/write. Reused via the stub, not built here.
relatedNotChildren.EUDPA-271: Type-ahead select — still noted as 'impacts' EUDPA-186; impact to resolve with the ticket owner (cv-026).

notes.authorities: The five rewritten Jira tickets (EUDPA-287/286/186/294/293) + EUDPA-295 are the requirements authority. The Standard Address Block field/validation table is the Confluence 'Live Animals Data Fields V4 — Common Attributes' page (id 6497338582, anchor #address_block; local mirror docs/confluence/live-animals-data-fields-v4/index.md lines 177-192). Decision IDs D3/D13/D21/D23/D24/D26 are cited by the tickets as a canonical decisions log — inlined under decisions{} from the ticket text (the standalone log was not in the extracts; the definitions here are ticket-sourced).
notes.topology: TWO new services + a workspace PR. (1) trade-imports-ins-frontend — a NET-NEW shared 'Import Notification Service' front-door (Node.js, CDP frontend template, GOV.UK header/footer/layout/start page; modelled on trade-imports-animals-frontend; does NOT absorb it). Hosts the address-book UI. (2) trade-imports-address-book — the system of record for addresses, produced by RENAMING trade-imports-operators IN PLACE (Sam-ruled cv-003, evolve + keep git history), modelled on trade-imports-reference-data (Java/Spring Boot + Mongo, NO messaging; ref-data pom has NO sqs/sns/kafka/ServiceBus deps — TOPOLOGY.referenceDataShape). animals-frontend keeps the journey select pages + review + submit gate. A FUTURE trade-imports-ins-backend is deferred — INS calls the Address Book API directly.
notes.apiStyle: REST per docs/best-practices/rest-api/ (noun paths): /organisation/{orgId}/addresses (list/create), /organisation/{orgId}/addresses/{id} (get/put/delete). JSON casing = camelCase (cv-001, ruled evidence-decisive): the reference model trade-imports-reference-data serves camelCase (Jackson default LOWER_CAMEL_CASE, no naming-strategy override — Country.java:15-16 code/name, CountriesControllerIT.java:57-58 asserts \"code\"/\"name\"), the ticket field vocabulary is already camelCase (countryCode, townOrCity, addressId, orgId), and it matches backend Origin.countryCode. This OVERTURNS the snake_case wire the RENAMED-FROM operators service serves today (JacksonConfig SNAKE_CASE + address_line_1 @JsonProperty) and the FE operators-client FIELD_MAP snake_case mapping — but ins-frontend is net-new and destructive-over-green is authorised (cv-004), so the client mapping is rewritten to camelCase rather than preserved. Pagination is a server-side CONFIG page size (default 25), NOT a query param.
notes.terminology: 'Operator' is RETIRED (D13, cv-015 supersedes v1 c-016). The address book stores ADDRESSES; a notification's addressId-reference + the role it plays is a CONSIGNMENT PARTY. UI copy, routes, model, API and tests all say 'address'/'consignment party'. This is a wide rename off the delivered build's pervasive 'operator' vocabulary.
notes.untyped: Addresses have NO type (D3, D21; cv-017 supersedes the v1 operator_type model). The service knows nothing about consignor/consignee/importer/role. The role is chosen when an address is SELECTED into a notification, not at save. One address can be consignor on one notification and consignee on the next (reference-not-copy). CONSEQUENCES on the renamed service: DELETE OperatorType enum + the 7 UI-ordered values (OperatorType.java:8-16), DELETE the operator_type query param (OperatorController.java:86), NO filter-by-type endpoint, NO type column on the list, drop the operatorType component from the crn_status_type_created index (Operator.java:31-33). The frontend's type-selection add page (animals-frontend add/type/controller.js:34-64) is REMOVED.
notes.referenceModel: Backend salvage target = trade-imports-operators renamed in place. KEEP (Operator.java): id, name, addressLine1/2, town->townOrCity, county, postcode, telephone->phone, email, status(tombstone), createdAt/modifiedAt. CHANGE country(:60, display-name)->countryCode (ISO alpha-2) — this REVERSES the operators-service D1/c-004 'do not fix' ruling (country/display-name Javadoc Operator.java:16-19; see cv-011). DROP operatorType(:46), transporterCategory, approvalNumber. RE-SCOPE crn->organisationId(:72) as the primary owner key; org_status index (Operator.java:34) becomes primary. Port unchanged: JacksonConfig (naming-strategy value flips to camelCase per cv-001), GlobalExceptionHandler, soft-delete/tombstone semantics + 404-vs-tombstone (OperatorService.java:139-141,209-222), pagination default 25/max 100, Pattern.quote server-side search (OperatorService.java:90), Testcontainers Mongo 7.0 harness (IntegrationBase.java:26-31). Frontend salvage into ins-frontend: address-book list/add/view/edit/delete controllers + views (animals-frontend src/server/address-book/*), operators-client CRUD (operators-client.js:158-295), operator-schema Joi (operator-schema.js) minus type/transporter branches, operator-countries GB-prepend (operator-countries.js:15-28), defra-id auth org-claim mapping (auth.js:45-50, MIRROR not move), build-navigation pattern (build-navigation.js). STALE v1 claims CORRECTED by the extracts: mock-consignors.json etc are ABSENT (selects already read the live operators API, not static mock JSON); formatAddress already renders county/telephone/email (does NOT drop them, notification-view-helper.js:32-52); outbox SCHEMA_VERSION is already '2' (OutboxService.java:25).
notes.freezeModel: EUDPA-295 freeze-on-submit reuses the EXISTING NotificationContentSnapshot machinery (NotificationContentSnapshot.java; Notification.java:24-25 @JsonIgnore submittedBaseline; NotificationService.java:317 set-on-amend, :338-344 restore-on-cancel). The change is WHEN/WHAT it captures: move capture from amend-start to SUBMIT, in the SAME transaction as the DRAFT/AMEND->SUBMITTED status change; keep addressId references alongside (additive, AC2); Submitted reads the baseline (AC3 — the @JsonIgnore must be revisited to make it a read source); amend re-resolves live (AC5); cancel restores the submit-time baseline (AC6). This also FIXES the amend-start capture bug that EUDPA-294's resolve-on-read introduces.
notes.scoping: Addresses are ORG-OWNED (D23; cv-010 supersedes v1 c-001). orgId comes from the Defra ID token claim currentRelationshipId, mapped to organisationId, read from the SESSION never the query string (auth.js:49; animals-frontend forwards Trade-Imports-Crn/Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id). API is PATH-scoped: /organisation/{orgId}/addresses. orgId is INDEXED and scopes EVERY read and write. D23 invariant: a signed-in user ALWAYS has an org — do NOT build null-org handling. cv-040 RULED: the API AUTHORISES the caller's forwarded session-org against the path orgId and returns 404 on a mismatch — the path is never trusted alone (identity-forwarding mechanism pinned at design alongside cv-042).
notes.salvage: See referenceModel. Every salvage claim is grounded in the REFERENCE CODE extracts with file:line. Repo of record for the backend rename is repos/trade-imports-operators (branch feat/EUDPA-58-address-book, verified present). ins-frontend does not exist yet (net-new). Sam's re-plan decisions (cv-002/003/004): stay on feat/EUDPA-58-address-book (no new branches), rename in place keeping history, go destructive over the green build (git-recoverable).

decisions.D3: Addresses have no type in the address book — the same address may be a consignor on one notification and a consignee on the next; a search page cannot be limited to a role (cited by EUDPA-294).
decisions.D13: The 'Operator' name is retired; the journey-side party is a 'consignment party' (cited by EUDPA-294).
decisions.D21: Addresses are untyped, so there is NO filter-by-type — text search is the only filter (cited by EUDPA-186).
decisions.D23: A signed-in user ALWAYS has an org (currentRelationshipId guaranteed present); do NOT build null-org handling (cited by EUDPA-287).
decisions.D24: placeOfOrigin stays INLINE in the journey, never a reference (cited by EUDPA-294/295).
decisions.D26: consignment (per-notification consignment-contact field) stays INLINE, never a reference (cited by EUDPA-294/295).

address.description: The untyped, org-owned Standard Address Block record — the address book's sole system-of-record row. camelCase on the wire (cv-001). Field validation table is unchanged from V4 / v1 (only country representation + field names changed).

fields.name: id
fields.derived: Server-assigned; returned on create (287.AC13); routes view/edit/delete (286.AC1/AC7); the selectable reference held by a notification (294.AC3). Ports from Operator.id.

fields.name: orgId
fields.derived: Owning organisation; from Defra ID currentRelationshipId->organisationId in session (auth.js:49); path segment /organisation/{orgId}/...; scopes every read/write (D23; cv-010). Re-scoped from Operator.organisationId:72 (crn demoted).

fields.name: name
fields.notes: V4 label 'Name or Organisation name'. Ports 1:1 from Operator.name.

fields.name: addressLine1
fields.notes: Ports from Operator.addressLine1. camelCase (was address_line_1 @JsonProperty on the snake_case wire).

fields.name: townOrCity
fields.notes: V4 'Town or city'; ticket field townOrCity. Operators service calls it 'town' (Operator.java) — renamed to townOrCity to match the ticket vocabulary.

fields.name: county
fields.notes: Optional; carries the GDS '(optional)' marker (cv-023 carries c-009). Ports from Operator.county.

fields.name: postcode
fields.notes: V4 'Postal or Zip code'. Ports from Operator.postcode.

fields.name: countryCode
fields.notes: Stored as ISO alpha-2, NOT validated against a country list on the API ('store what you are given'; matches Origin.countryCode). The FE picker is MDM-sourced (reference-data /countries, already alpha-2) so invalid codes shouldn't arrive. REVERSES the operators-service D1/c-004 display-name ruling (Operator.java:60 + country display-name Javadoc :16-19 'do not fix'). Picker/store validation asymmetry recorded at cv-044.

fields.name: phone
fields.notes: V4 'Telephone number'; ticket field 'phone'. Ports from Operator.telephone (renamed telephone->phone to match ticket).

fields.name: deleted
fields.derived: Added by EUDPA-286; soft-deleted rows are excluded from list/search/selection but still resolvable by id (286.AC7). Ports from the OperatorStatus ACTIVE/DELETED tombstone (Operator.java:74, OperatorStatus.java:8-11) — reshape to a boolean flag or keep a status enum; behaviour is the tombstone.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.derived: Audit / list ordering. Ports from Operator.createdAt:76 (@CreatedDate).

fields.name: modifiedAt
fields.derived: Audit only (nothing re-syncs off it). Ports from Operator.modifiedAt:80 (@LastModifiedDate).

droppedFromOperatorModel: crn (Operator.java:70 — demoted from primary scope key; organisationId is now primary, cv-010)

consignmentParty.description: Journey-side (animals-backend) role field. A notification's reference-plus-role. 'Operator' name retired (D13, cv-015). Six party fields sit on NotificationBase.java:31-41 (typed Operator today) + transport.transporter (Transport.java:22).
consignmentParty.referenceableRolesNote: These hold an addressId reference and resolve current details on read (294.AC3/AC4). NARROWED from v1: cv-014 supersedes c-014 which had placeOfOrigin + consignment-contact(BRANCH_ADDRESS) + transporter in the select-page set.

inlineOnlyRoles.note: Entered inline in the journey; NEVER a reference; already frozen. Inverts v1 j-002 (which swapped it to an operator select).
Per-notification consignment-contact field, inline; NEVER a reference; already frozen. Inverts v1 j-004/c-014 (which mapped it to a BRANCH_ADDRESS operator).

consignmentParty.transporter: DEFERRED out of the address book (cv-013). Stays the inline Transporter class (Transport.java:22 — operatorId,name,telephone,email,Address,approvalNumber,type). Not address-book-backed; approval number is found via the approved-transporter list (EUDPA-119), not typed. Nothing in this scope handles it.
consignmentParty.snapshotCoexistence: On submit, EUDPA-295 captures a frozen snapshot of the RESOLVED details AND retains the addressId (additive — reference is never dropped). Submitted reads the snapshot; amend re-resolves live. Reuses NotificationContentSnapshot/submittedBaseline (see notes.freezeModel).

pages.purpose: INS shell service navigation with TWO links — 'Dashboard' and 'Address book'; selecting 'Address book' opens the address book. Built on the CDP template default (GOV.UK header/footer/layout/start page), no bespoke shell this ticket. Dashboard target/content is out of scope here (future unified dashboard).
pages.derived: Mirrors build-navigation.js pattern (Dashboard/Address book/About, current-flag) from animals-frontend (build-navigation.js:1-21). Absorbs EUDPA-185.AC1/AC2 (cv-028 carries c-015).

pages.purpose: Sign in through the Defra ID STUB exactly as animals-frontend; reach a signed-in page; session exposes the organisation (currentRelationshipId->organisationId); sign-out returns to the start page.
pages.derived: MIRROR (not move) auth.js:45-50 org-claim mapping + defraId config block (config.js:175-246, DEFRA_ID_* env). Stub OIDC discovery at :3007. D23: never build null-org handling.

pages.purpose: Org-scoped address book landing: table of EVERY address belonging to my org showing Name, Address, Country; 'Add a new address' action; pagination; empty state; search (186).
components: govuk-table columns Name / Address (single column — composition per openItems oi-address-column, recommended addressLine1, townOrCity, postcode joined by commas) / Country; NO operator-type column (untyped); soft-deleted excluded (deleted flag)
Empty-state message + add action when no addresses (287.AC7 — copy unspecified)
Persistent one-shot govukNotificationBanner (success), cleared on next navigation, NO timed dismiss (cv-021 carries c-006; AC '3 seconds' to be amended)
Search input (govuk-frontend search component) name/townOrCity/postcode/countryCode, server-side ?q= (cv-026); result count; clear-search (186.AC1/AC4)
No-search-results state (DISTINCT from the empty-state): 'no addresses match "<term>"' message echoing the submitted term back into the search input + clear-search action (186.AC4, b-server-search)
pages.derived: Salvage animals-frontend address-book/list/* (controller.js:108 one-shot banner; numbered variant via buildPaginationLinks(...) with a buildQueryString arg (notification-helper.js:141-191), rendered through govukPagination — NOT server/home/_pagination.njk, which is the home/dashboard PREVIOUS/NEXT-only partial) — STRIP the operator-type filter column/options; rename 'operator'->'address'. Absorbs EUDPA-185.AC3/AC4.

pages.purpose: 'Add address details' page — enter the Standard Address Block fields and Save. NO type-selection page precedes it (removed — cv-017). Country picked from MDM (reference-data /countries), stored as countryCode.
components: Standard Address Block fields per entities.address (name, addressLine1, addressLine2 optional, townOrCity, county optional-marker, postcode, country picker, phone, email)
Country select from operator-countries GB-prepend (operator-countries.js:15-28) over the reference-data SPS list; throws on empty rather than degrading
Buttons 'Save changes' + 'Cancel'; error summary + inline errors (287.AC9)
Manual entry only; address-lookup out of scope (EUDPA-290; cv-022 carries c-008)
pages.derived: Salvage animals-frontend address-book/add/details/* + operator-schema.js MINUS operatorType requirement and the TRANSPORTER-conditional approvalNumber/transporterCategory branches (cv-013). REMOVE the add/type page entirely (add/type/controller.js:34-64).

pages.purpose: Address details page — name + contact details + full address (read-only) with Edit and Delete actions (286.AC1).
pages.derived: Salvage animals-frontend address-book/view/*. The prototype already routes by operatorId (an id, not a slug) — src/server/address-book/index.js:40/45/55 use /address-book/{operatorId} (+ /edit, /delete), so it ports directly to the new by-id routes; there is no slug to reconcile.

pages.purpose: 'Edit address details' — every field pre-populated + editable; Save changes persists (full-replace PUT), Cancel returns to the Address book page WITHOUT saving (286.AC2/AC3).
components: Same field set as add, prefilled from GET by-id
Full-replace semantics: omitted optionals are CLEARED not preserved (286.AC7)
Success banner on save (cv-021); error summary + inline (286.AC4)
pages.derived: Salvage animals-frontend address-book/edit/*; same untyped simplification as add.

pages.purpose: Confirmation interstitial before the soft delete (286.AC5). Cancel returns to the address details page (no-op); Delete soft-deletes and returns to the Address book page with a deletion confirmation.
pages.derived: cv-024 carries c-010 — now AC-backed (286.AC5 explicitly 'prompts for confirmation'). Salvage animals-frontend address-book/delete/*.

apiOperations.purpose: Return that org's addresses, paginated 25/page (config, NOT a query param), excluding soft-deleted; extended with ?q= server-side text search (186).

params.orgId: path — organisation scope (from session, never query string)
params.q: optional free-text; server-side OR-match over name/townOrCity/postcode/countryCode (186.AC2 — searched set narrowed to these 4). For the 'country' term the typed value is resolved against the MDM country list to its ISO alpha-2 code BEFORE matching, so a trader typing 'France' matches stored 'FR' (adopted option (a) of cv-048; the field set is confirm-with-UX). case-insensitive + partial-word (186.AC3 — regex/prefix, NOT Mongo $text whole-word; see cv-047)
params.page: optional page number; SAME single pagination rule as base list (186.AC7)

responses.200: top-level object {items,page,pageSize,totalItems,totalPages} (camelCase, cv-001); DELETED excluded; org isolation enforced in the DB query not post-filter (186.AC5)

apiOperations.behaviours: Page size mirrors animals-backend notification.list.page-size:25 read via @Value + PageRequest.of (NotificationService.java:85-86,217). Server-side search reuses the case-insensitive Pattern.quote regex MECHANISM (OperatorService.java:90 / OperatorRepository.java:48-58) but DELIBERATELY NARROWS the matched-field set from the salvaged 7 fields (name, addressLine1, addressLine2, town, county, postcode, country) to the 4 the AC names — name/townOrCity/postcode/countryCode — dropping addressLine1/2 and county from search; the type $in clause is DROPPED. This is a reduction from the salvaged query, not a straight port. countryCode matching is fed by resolving a typed country name to its alpha-2 code first (cv-048, 186.AC2).

apiOperations.purpose: Create an address, persist to MongoDB scoped to that org, return 201 with its id. 400 naming EACH field at fault on invalid. MUST NOT accept a type or role field.

responses.400: problem+json with per-field errors map (mandatory-missing + over-max), naming each offending field; single-source validation shared with the form (287.AC9/AC13)

apiOperations.behaviours: Untyped: body carries NO type/role (cv-017). countryCode stored as-given, not list-validated (cv-011). Bean Validation @NotBlank/@Size ports from OperatorRequest MINUS operatorType/@ValidTransporterFields. cv-040 RULED: the API authorises session-org against the path orgId (404 on mismatch). cv-044 RULED (mixed): validate mandatory + max-length, REJECT a stray type/role field with a per-field 400, add email format validation; phone and countryCode are NOT format/list-validated.

apiOperations.purpose: Return a single address INCLUDING a soft-deleted one (flagged), so notifications can resolve old references. 404 for unknown id. An id belonging to another org must not be returned.

responses.200: address, including soft-deleted flagged as deleted — the ONLY endpoint that surfaces deleted rows (286.AC7; 294.AC2 asymmetry: list hides deleted, by-id returns it)
responses.404: unknown id; cross-org id not returned (recommend 404 not 403 to avoid existence disclosure — openItems oi-cross-org-status)

apiOperations.behaviours: Ports OperatorService get-includes-tombstone (OperatorService.java:139-141). Resolve-on-read source for 294.AC4 (drafts show latest) and the deleted signal for 293.

apiOperations.purpose: FULL REPLACE — every field from the body; an omitted optional is CLEARED not left unchanged. Persist scoped to org. 404 unknown/cross-org. Org-wide edit (affects every user).
apiOperations.behaviours: PUT not PATCH; full-replace clears omitted optionals (286.AC7 crossCutting). Ports OperatorService update, minus the operatorType immutability check (:171-174).

apiOperations.purpose: Set the deleted flag, return 204, WITHOUT removing the record. Org-wide (disappears for the whole org). Excluded from list/search + notification-creation selection thereafter; still resolvable by-id.
apiOperations.behaviours: Ports OperatorService soft-delete (status->DELETED, idempotent, OperatorService.java:209-222). UI reaches it only via the confirmation page (cv-024).

behaviours.description: Addresses have NO type/role anywhere in the address book (D3/D21). No operatorType field, no type column, no operator_type query param, no filter-by-type endpoint. The type-selection add page is removed. Supersedes the entire v1 typed model.

Addresses belong to the organisation, not the creating user — immediately available to every user in the org (287.AC11), org-wide edit/delete (286.AC6). orgId indexed, scopes every read/write; enforced in the DB query, not post-filter (186.AC5).

DELETE sets a deleted flag (never removes the row); excluded from list/search/selection; still resolvable by-id (flagged). Ports the operators tombstone (Operator.java:74; OperatorService.java:209-222).

The Standard Address Block field/validation rules are the ONE authoritative source (the V4 'Common Attributes' table). Because the split is Java (API Bean Validation) + Node (form Joi), the accepted interpretation is TWO mirrored rule sets — Node Joi mirrors Java Bean Validation, both derived from the same V4 table — kept honest by a parity/convention test that asserts identical field maxLengths and mandatory flags across the two. There is no runtime-shared schema; 'must not drift' is enforced by that parity test, not a single shared artifact.

countryCode is stored as given (ISO alpha-2), NOT validated against a country list on the API. The FE picker is MDM-sourced so invalid codes shouldn't arrive. Reverses c-004/D1 (cv-011). Asymmetry recorded cv-044.

Server-side text search only (186), extending the base list endpoint with ?q= (no separate endpoint, no client-side filtering). Matches over 4 fields — name/townOrCity/postcode/countryCode (186.AC2); the 'country' term is resolved against the MDM country list to its alpha-2 code before matching so a typed country NAME matches the stored code (cv-048). Case-insensitive + partial-word via a case-insensitive regex $or (Pattern.quote, ReDoS-safe), NOT a Mongo $text index (whole-word/stemmed) — cv-047 RULED. Zero-result SEARCH is a DISTINCT render path from the org-has-no-addresses empty-state (b-empty-state): show a 'no addresses match "<term>"' message that ECHOES the user's search term back into the search input so they can correct it, plus a clear-search action (186.AC4). Same single pagination rule (25) as base list (186.AC7).

Page size (default 25) is a server-side CONFIG property, not a query param. FE renders the page it is handed (do not hard-code 25 or pass a size). Mirror animals-backend notification.list.page-size @Value + PageRequest.of (NotificationService.java:85-86,217); render the numbered variant via buildPaginationLinks(...) with a buildQueryString arg (notification-helper.js:141-191) + govukPagination. server/home/_pagination.njk is the home prev/next-only partial, NOT the address-book numbered source.

On save (add/edit) return to /address-book with a success banner; delete shows a deletion confirmation; the new/updated address appears in the list. The OPERATIVE confirmation behaviour of 287.AC10 / 286.AC3 (return-to-list, success confirmation, item in list) is SATISFIED. The only clause NOT delivered as literally written is 'visible for exactly 3 seconds' — an unusual GDS pattern; cv-021 (carrying c-006) RULES a persistent one-shot govukNotificationBanner cleared on next navigation with NO timed dismiss, and records the '3 seconds' AC wording to be amended. This is a KNOWING, ruled deviation pending AC amendment, not an omission — the ticket owner accepts/amends the 3s clause. Salvage the one-shot flash (list/controller.js:108).
Two-step delete with a confirmation interstitial (286.AC5): Cancel -> details page (no-op); Confirm -> soft-delete -> list + confirmation. cv-024 carries c-010, now AC-backed.
First 25 then paginate when >25; numbered govukPagination presentation preserving ?q=. Search paging preserves the active term (186.AC7). Page authoritative from server (cv-025).
When the org has saved no addresses, show a 'no addresses yet' message + the add action (287.AC7). Copy unspecified. Distinct render path from the table.

Cancel on the add page discards input, navigates back to the list, no POST issued (287.AC12). Discard confirmation-guard: recommend NO confirm (GDS Cancel discards silently) — openItems oi-discard-confirm.

journeyIntegration.description: The journey's party search pages show the org's REAL address-book addresses (every address the org saved, org-wide not per-user), exclude soft-deleted, select by addressId, and remove any placeholder/hardcoded source (294.AC1/AC2). SCOPE narrowed to the referenceable roles only: consignor/consignee/importer/destination (the NotificationBase.destination field, NotificationBase.java:31-41). placeOfOrigin (D24) + consignment (D26) are INLINE — NOT select pages (cv-014). STALE v1 CORRECTED: mock-consignors.json etc are ABSENT — the five select controllers already read the LIVE operators API (operatorsClient.listOperators), select by operator id not array index (consignors/select/controller.js:64-66). So 'delete the hardcoded JSON' is largely already done; verify no residual placeholder seed. The select controllers repoint to the INS Address Book API (org-scoped path) and stop type-filtering.

A role field holds EITHER an addressId reference (resolve current details on read) OR an inline address (place of origin) — a sum-type (294.AC5). Draft/Amend resolve-on-read gives latest details with no trader action (294.AC4); NO push/fan-out into drafts (explicitly a bug). SUPERSEDES v1 c-003/c-017 copy+reference+staleness-accepted (cv-012): 293.AC1 (edited reaches draft) is now MET BY DESIGN. Model/class layout is the dev's call. Resolution needs a NEW client method that returns address VALUES — the existing OperatorsApiClient.classify() is deliberately values-free (classify-only, c-017 legacy), so a hydrating client/type is new work. PERFORMANCE: resolve-on-read is one Address Book API call per referenced consignment party, so notification LIST views become N notifications x parties calls (N+1) — the ticket cross-notes flag 'expect to need caching on list views'; measure and batch/cache resolution on list views (keep the no-fan-out-into-drafts rule, but pair it with a list-view caching expectation).

EUDPA-295 freeze-on-submit (backend-only): capture the RESOLVED address details as a frozen snapshot in the SAME transaction as DRAFT/AMEND->SUBMITTED (AC1); retain addressId alongside, additive (AC2); Submitted reads the snapshot not a live resolve (AC3); post-submit edit/delete does not change the submitted view and must not error even on a dangling id (AC4); amend re-resolves live then behaves as Draft (AC5); cancel-amend restores the submit-time snapshot (AC6). Reuse NotificationContentSnapshot/submittedBaseline (NotificationContentSnapshot.java; Notification.java:24-25; NotificationService.java:317,338-344) — MOVE capture from amend-start to submit; revisit the @JsonIgnore on submittedBaseline to make it a read source (AC3). Two role fields already frozen and need nothing: placeOfOrigin (D24), consignment (D26). Hard gate before go-live. SEQUENCING (coordinate with ji-deleted-gate / EUDPA-293): 293's deleted-address validation and this snapshot capture mutate the SAME submit transaction. Whichever of 293/295 lands second MUST insert its step so guardOperatorExistenceForSubmit (the deleted-gate) runs BEFORE the snapshot capture — otherwise a soft-deleted address gets frozen into the legal record, the exact outcome the ordering prevents. Add a submit-flow integration test asserting validate-then-freeze order: a submit referencing a deleted address is rejected AND no snapshot is written.

Deleted-address submit gate. AC1 (FE review page): resolving a consignment party returns a soft-deleted address -> summary message 'select a replacement' + inline message per affected role + block submit; lives WITH the existing review-page validation. AC2 (BE authoritative): reject a submit that references a soft-deleted address REGARDLESS of frontend (direct API call), name each affected role, status stays unchanged; clean submit succeeds. AC3 (FE replace): inline message is a link to the relevant part of the notification; choosing a valid replacement clears it; submit once none remain. Submit-flow ordering LOAD-BEARING: this deleted-address validation runs BEFORE the EUDPA-295 snapshot ('validate then freeze'). SEQUENCING (coordinate with ji-freeze-submit / EUDPA-295): the blockingGraph does not force a 293<->295 build order, so whichever lands second must insert its step preserving validate-then-freeze; a submit-flow integration test pins it (deleted-ref submit rejected AND no snapshot written). Detection = resolve-returns-soft-deleted (294 semantics). Salvage the existing fail-closed submit guard (NotificationService.java:258-304 guardOperatorExistenceForSubmit — DELETED leg = 293.AC2) + the declaration submit-error mapping (declaration/controller.js:19-36,85-97 — the v1 'review-page pre-check' is really a submit-time error mapping). Supersedes the v1 existence-check-only mechanism (cv-016): values are now resolved.

EUDPA-198 (absorbed into 294 as a DUPLICATE): the Standard Address Block must be representable on the notification model. ALREADY realised on feat/EUDPA-58-address-book — Address.java has county:18 + postcode:19; Operator/Transporter carry telephone+email (verified: grep Address.java). formatAddress already renders county/telephone/email (notification-view-helper.js:32-52) — v1 c-020's 'silently dropped' claim is STALE/realised (cv-030). Outstanding 198 delta = DROP addressLine3 (Address.java:16 still present, verified) — a breaking removal rippling into builders/DTO/mappers/NotificationSubmittedData/fixtures; verify no consumer reads addressLine3 first.

NotificationSubmittedData ships full party PII (name/phone/email/full Address incl postcode/county) to Dynamics/ASB (OutboxService.java:39-40; NotificationSubmittedData.java:10-42). SCHEMA_VERSION is ALREADY '2' (OutboxService.java:25) — v1 c-021's '1->2 bump' is realised (cv-031). If the reference/resolve reshape or the addressLine3 drop changes the emitted operator/address shape, bump to '3' + pin with an integration test. Comms/DPIA obligation (PII) to the downstream consumer stands.

EUDPA-287.AC1 requires a SEPARATE workspace PR adding BOTH services. Touch: docker/stack/backend.compose.yml (declare address-book as a sibling of reference-data — profiles/backend, depends_on mongodb, health curl, image, port; ref-data uses 8086, operators targeted at 8089), docker/stack/dev.compose.yml (build-from-source stanza target dev-run + src volume mount, mirroring ref-data lines 54-63), Makefile REPOS + JAVA_REPOS (add trade-imports-address-book; add ins-frontend to REPOS + NODE_REPOS), CLAUDE.md repo map (two new rows). BOTH services get a dev-overlay build-from-source + src-mount stanza in dev.compose.yml: the Java address-book mirrors ref-data lines 54-63 (target dev-run, hot-reload via Spring Boot DevTools), and ins-frontend gets the equivalent Node dev overlay (build-from-source + src volume mount + hot-reload) rather than just being added to NODE_REPOS. NEVER edit .staged/ (generated). Mongo seed goes via the tests repo (init-scripts.sh:91), not docker/stack. NAMING (cv-042 RULED): rename the wiring to trade-imports-address-book / TRADE_IMPORTS_ADDRESS_BOOK_URL / mongo db trade-imports-address-book / seed renamed off 30-operators-seed.js; KEEP port 8089 and the Trade-Imports-Organisation-Id header name; ins-frontend gets its own DEFRA_ID_* block + address-book-URL config. The two uncommitted TRADE_IMPORTS_OPERATORS_URL compose edits become TRADE_IMPORTS_ADDRESS_BOOK_URL. Both services must boot from PUBLISHED IMAGES (chicken-and-egg: CI must publish first — see ji-cicd-publish) AND hot-reload under make docker-compose-dev.

Per-repo CI/CD image publishing — the PREREQUISITE that makes ji-workspace-wiring's image: references (and 287.AC1 'boot from published images') meetable, and that gives cross-repo branch-tag parity (CLAUDE.md rule 2) an image to pick up. (1) trade-imports-ins-frontend is net-new with NO pipeline: add its GitHub Actions Docker build+publish workflow to Dockerhub (branch-tagged + :latest). (2) The rename-in-place (cv-003) changes the published image name from trade-imports-operators to trade-imports-address-book: rename the operators image build accordingly so branch-tagged and :latest images exist under the new name before the workspace PR sets image:. Without published images the workspace PR wiring and AC1 are unmeetable. Branch-parity tag requirement applies to both.

EUDPA-185.note: Absorbed into EUDPA-287 (pendingDisposition). Mapped to 287 equivalents.

EUDPA-287.note: Skeleton — stands up both services, sign-in, list, add, POST + the shared Standard Address Block. blockedBy EUDPA-59 as a CONFIG/REUSE prerequisite: 287.AC2 sign-in + the org-in-session it depends on reuse the Defra ID stub (mirrored auth.js), same reuse EUDPA-294 depends on — so 59 gates both, not only 294. Also needs ji-cicd-publish so published images exist for AC1.

EUDPA-286.note: Adds by-id GET, full-replace PUT, soft-DELETE + the deleted flag. The BULK of 286 is blockedBy only 287, BUT AC8 (edits reach drafts, never submitted notifications) is untestable until 294+295 land — it is PARTIALLY blockedBy EUDPA-295's snapshot. Do NOT close 286 as done on the strength of 287 alone; AC8 stays open until 295 exists (treat AC8 as a follow-on increment gated behind 295).

EUDPA-186.note: Extends the list endpoint with ?q=; excludes deleted (needs 286's flag). 'Impacted by' EUDPA-271.

EUDPA-294.note: Reference + resolve-on-read; absorbs EUDPA-198. Introduces the amend-start capture bug that 295 fixes.

EUDPA-293.note: Deleted-address submit gate; no hard build-order dependency on 295, but the two mutate the SAME submit transaction and MUST preserve validate-then-freeze ordering. SEQUENCING HAZARD: if 295 is wired before 293's gate, a soft-deleted address can be snapshotted into the legal record. Whichever lands second inserts its step so the deleted-gate runs before the snapshot; pin with a submit-flow integration test (deleted-ref submit rejected AND no snapshot written). See ji-deleted-gate + ji-freeze-submit.

EUDPA-295.note: Freeze-on-submit snapshot; hard gate before go-live; fixes 294's amend-start capture bug. EUDPA-286.AC8 cannot pass without it. SEQUENCING HAZARD with 293 (same submit transaction): capture the snapshot only AFTER the deleted-address gate has run (validate-then-freeze); see the 293 coordination note + ji-freeze-submit.

openItems.issue: The single 'Address' table column's composition — which of addressLine1/addressLine2/townOrCity/county/postcode render, and how — is a design decision the ticket leaves open.
openItems.recommendation: Render the address block joined by commas; minimum addressLine1, townOrCity, postcode. Confirm with UX/BA.

openItems.issue: For an id that exists but belongs to another org, the endpoint 'must not return' it — the 404-vs-403 status code choice.
openItems.recommendation: SETTLED consistent with cv-040 (RULED): return 404 (not 403) on an org mismatch so a caller cannot distinguish 'exists in another org' from 'does not exist' — no existence disclosure.

openItems.issue: Whether Cancel/discard on the add (or edit) page needs a confirmation guard before dropping entered input is unspecified.
openItems.recommendation: No confirmation — GDS Cancel discards silently and returns to the list. Confirm with UX.
